summary:
Introduced in the House of Representatives on April 7, 2025, by Representative Pettersen and Representative Nunn, H.R. 2695, known as the "Communities of Recovery Reauthorization Act of 2025," proposes to amend the Public Health Service Act. The primary purpose of the bill is to reauthorize and increase funding for federal grants dedicated to building communities of recovery.

The bill's major provision modifies Section 547(f) of the Public Health Service Act (codified at 42 U.S.C. 290ee-2(f)). Under the existing law, the program was authorized to receive appropriations of $5,000,000 for each fiscal year from 2019 through 2023. The proposed amendment strikes this outdated funding structure and replaces it with an authorized appropriation of $17,000,000 for each fiscal year from 202
